description.title: Investment-in-cycle: an irreversible clean-tech adoption that closes inside the joint loop
description.economic_framing: Two coupled carbon markets A<->B (as in joint_two_market.json). A compliance firm in market A can make an IRREVERSIBLE investment in a cheaper abatement technology (block cost L=40 vs the incumbent H=100). Whether it pays to invest depends on market A's allowance price, which in turn depends on the COUPLED market B's price through the cross-market cost link. So the adoption decision is nested INSIDE the joint price fixed point: the firm's Dixit-Pindyck trigger is evaluated on each outer sweep against the current neighbour-driven price, and once it crosses it adopts and stays adopted (a monotone floor).
description.mechanism: trigger_mode='break_even' (M=1) so the option adopts once market A's delivered price reaches theta=130 USD/tCO2. On the cold seed (back-link B->A cut) A only sees the incumbent block, so its seed price is H=100 < theta and it does NOT adopt on sweep 1. As B's price feeds back into A, A's un-adopted joint price rises toward 157.1 > theta, so A crosses the trigger on a LATER sweep, DRIVEN by the coupled market -- the genuine investment-in-cycle witness. After adoption the joint fixed point falls to the ADOPTED prices (A depresses its own price below theta -> ex-post regret, which the irreversibility floor permits, docs/joint-equilibrium.md section 4).
description.hand_solution: phi=0.4 both directions, c_B=80. Un-adopted joint fixed point (incumbent H=100): P_A=157.14, P_B=142.86. Adopted joint fixed point (option L=40): P_A=85.71, P_B=114.29. theta=130 sits between H (seed) and the un-adopted P_A, so the firm adopts via the coupled feedback and the market lands on the ADOPTED pair.

expected_output.reading: Investment bites: the joint price pair moves from the un-adopted (157.1, 142.9) to the adopted (85.7, 114.3) because A's firm crossed its Dixit-Pindyck trigger on a neighbour-driven sweep. The adoption is recorded and the loop converges (Joint Converged=1) with no cycle.
